steps.title: Substitute and collect to standard form
steps.content: Set $y = x - c$ and $y = -6(x-12)^2$ equal: $x - c = -6(x-12)^2$. Expand and rearrange: $6x^2 - 143x + (864-c) = 0$.

steps.title: Discriminant shortcut for two distinct solutions
steps.content: Two distinct real intersections require $\Delta > 0$: $(-143)^2 - 4(6)(864-c) > 0$, giving $20449 - 20736 + 24c > 0$, so $c > 287/24 \approx 11.96$.

steps.title: Check choices and match
steps.content: Choice C gives $c = 287/24$ exactly — discriminant $= 0$, tangent only. Choice D gives $c = 24 > 11.96$, so $\Delta > 0$: two distinct intersections. That matches choice D — D is correct.
